content.claim: Hungary tracks students earlier and more intensively than the OECD average: approximately 85% of Hungarian students are in schools that can select by academic performance, compared to the OECD average of 43%
content.context: Fresh live research through the International comparison lens
limitations: Based on school-level selection capacity rather than actual sorting outcomes
Does not distinguish between formal policy and de facto practice
Point-in-time comparison without longitudinal trend data
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
